Trustee Request for Proposal
Independent ESOP Trustee Selection

{{companyName}}
Transaction Size

Response Deadline
{{responseDeadline}}
§ 01Transaction Overview

{{companyName}} is a {{industry}} company based in {{city}}, {{state}}, employing {{employeeCount}} people. The company has been in operation since {{foundingYear}} and specializes in {{specialization}}.

The owner is pursuing a 100% sale to an Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) trust. The projected adjusted EBITDA is {{projectedEbitda}}, with an anticipated transaction value of {{transactionSize}}. The transaction is expected to close by {{targetCloseDate}}.

{{industry}}
{{employeeCount}}
{{projectedEbitda}}
{{transactionSize}}
{{targetCloseDate}}
§ 02Scope of Engagement

The selected trustee will serve as the independent fiduciary for the ESOP trust, responsible for the following:

A.Valuation review. Evaluate the independent FMV appraisal prepared by the valuation firm. Assess methodology, assumptions, and conclusions. Negotiate price on behalf of the ESOP if the appraisal supports a lower valuation than the proposed purchase price.
B.Transaction approval. Review and approve all material transaction terms, including the purchase agreement, seller note terms, SBA loan terms, and any related-party arrangements. Represent the interests of ESOP participants throughout negotiations.
C.Due diligence. Conduct independent due diligence on the company's financial condition, operations, management, and prospects. Review QofE findings, tax returns, and material contracts.
D.Closing participation. Execute all trust-related closing documents on behalf of the ESOP. Confirm that all conditions precedent to closing have been satisfied.
E.Post-close duties. Provide ongoing oversight of the ESOP trust, including annual valuation reviews, distribution policy administration, repurchase obligation monitoring, and compliance with ERISA requirements.
§ 03Independence Requirements

The trustee must satisfy all independence standards under ERISA and DOL guidance. Specifically:

A.ERISA §408(b)(2) compliance. The trustee must qualify as an independent fiduciary under ERISA and provide a written disclosure of all direct and indirect compensation in connection with the engagement.
B.No prior relationships. The trustee and its affiliates must have no existing business, personal, or financial relationship with the company, its owner(s), Forhemit, or any member of the deal team that could impair independence.
C.Conflict disclosure. The trustee must disclose all potential conflicts of interest, including any prior engagements involving the company, its industry, or any party to the transaction. Any conflict that cannot be cured will disqualify the candidate.
D.DOL standards. The trustee must demonstrate compliance with the Department of Labor's independence standards, including the ability to withstand scrutiny under a DOL examination or prohibited transaction claim.
Important: Independence is non-negotiable. Proposals that do not include a complete independence disclosure will not be considered.
§ 04Prudent Process Mandate

The selected trustee must document a prudent process for every material decision. At minimum, the trustee's file must include:

A.Due diligence steps. A contemporaneous record of all due diligence performed, including documents reviewed, interviews conducted, and analyses completed.
B.Valuation review methodology. Documentation of how the FMV appraisal was evaluated, including any independent analysis performed and the basis for accepting or challenging the valuation conclusion.
C.Negotiation record. A written record of all negotiations conducted on behalf of the ESOP, including offers, counteroffers, and the rationale for the final terms accepted.
D.Basis for approval. A written memorandum explaining why the trustee concluded that the transaction is in the best interest of ESOP participants and beneficiaries, considering the totality of the circumstances.
E.Ongoing monitoring framework. A description of how the trustee will monitor the ESOP's investment on an ongoing basis, including triggers for additional review or action.
Standard: The trustee should be prepared to produce this documentation in response to a DOL examination or participant complaint. Prudent process documentation is the trustee's primary defense.
§ 05Fee Structure Request

Please provide a complete fee proposal covering the following:

{{initialFee}}
{{feeStructure}}
{{retainerAmount}}
{{milestonePayments}}
{{postCloseAnnualFee}}
{{travelExpenses}}

Indicate whether fees are fixed or hourly. If hourly, provide rates for each professional who may work on the engagement. All fees and expenses must be disclosed in accordance with ERISA §408(b)(2).

§ 06Required Qualifications

Respondents must demonstrate the following qualifications:

A.ESOP trustee experience. Minimum {{minimumYears}} years serving as independent ESOP trustee, with at least {{minimumTransactions}} completed transactions.
B.Valuation expertise. Demonstrated ability to evaluate ERISA-compliant FMV appraisals and negotiate purchase prices on behalf of ESOP trusts.
C.ERISA counsel relationship. Access to independent ERISA counsel for legal guidance on fiduciary matters, prohibited transactions, and DOL compliance.
D.DOL examination history. Disclosure of any DOL examinations, investigations, or enforcement actions involving the firm or its principals in the past 10 years.
E.Professional liability insurance. Current professional liability (E&O) insurance with minimum coverage of {{insuranceMinimum}}.
F.Industry experience. Preference given to candidates with prior experience in the {{industry}} sector or with companies of comparable size and complexity.
§ 07Response Requirements

Your proposal should include the following materials:

1.Firm overview. History, organizational structure, office locations, and number of professionals dedicated to ESOP trustee services.
2.Team bios. Resumes of the lead trustee and any professionals who will be directly involved in the engagement.
3.Relevant experience. Summary of 5–10 comparable ESOP trustee engagements, including transaction size, industry, and outcome.
4.Fee proposal. Complete fee schedule per § 05 above.
5.Independence disclosure. Written confirmation of independence or disclosure of any potential conflicts per § 03 above.
6.References. Minimum three (3) professional references from companies or advisors for whom you have served as ESOP trustee within the past 5 years.
Submit to: Proposals should be delivered to deals@forhemit.com with the subject line "Trustee RFP Response — {{companyName}}" by {{responseDeadline}}.

Forhemit reserves the right to reject any or all proposals, waive informalities, and negotiate terms with one or more respondents. This RFP does not obligate Forhemit or the company to engage any respondent.
